target.title: fix(ui): reconcile model dropdown cache with server-resolved model after sessions.patch
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: The WebChat model dropdown shows the user-selected model (e.g. `codex/gpt-5.5`) while the server silently falls back to a different provider/model (e.g. `ollama/qwen3.5:9b` for unreachable Codex). The `/model` slash command already reconciles with `patched.resolved`, but the dropdown path never consumed the same data. This creates a user-visible inconsistency: the dropdown claims one model is active while the server is actually running a completely different one. Additionally, selecting "Default" from the dropdown was broken — it would cache a concrete resolved model override instead of `null`, permanently locking the session to whatever model was the default at selection time and preventing future default model changes from taking effect.

**Solution**: Read `patched.resolved` from the `sessions.patch` response and reconcile `chatModelOverrides` with the server-resolved model. Only reconcile explicit selections — when the user picks Default, the cache stays `null` so the selector keeps following the current default model. The fix is gated behind `if (nextModel)` which is falsy only for the empty string produced by the Default selection. A `JSON.stringify` comparison prevents redundant state updates when the resolved model matches the requested one — avoiding unnecessary React re-renders.

**What changed**: `ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts` — +40/-3. In `switchChatModel`, after awaiting `sessions.patch`, read `patched.resolved.model` and `patched.resolved.modelProvider`, call `resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue` + `createChatModelOverride`, compare with `JSON.stringify` against the current override, and update the cache only if different. Gated behind `if (nextModel)`. Imported `resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue` from `chat-model-ref.ts` and `SessionsPatchResult` type from `types.ts`.

**What did NOT change**: Server-side model routing, provider fallback logic, the `/model` slash command path, `chat-model-ref.ts`, any other UI module, config, auth, protocol, or SDK surface. The existing `catch` block that restores `prevOverride` on RPC failure is preserved unmodified, maintaining error-recovery semantics unchanged.

Fixes #93346

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: The model dropdown shows the user-selected model while the server silently falls back to a different provider/model, hiding the effective runtime model from the user. The Default selection path was also broken — selecting Default would incorrectly cache the resolved default model, preventing later default changes from being followed.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 24.13, Linux x86_64
- **Branch**: `fix/issue-93346` at `a8a6dee7b8f`
- **Test runner**: `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` + direct `node --import tsx/esm` import of `createChatModelOverride` and `resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue`
- **Browser**: Google Chrome (headless) via Playwright 1.60, loading PR branch build via Vite

**Root cause**: `switchChatModel` immediately writes `createChatModelOverride(nextModel)` into the cache before the RPC completes. After `sessions.patch` returns, the server-resolved model in `patched.resolved` is never consumed — the response payload is silently discarded. The `/model` command at `slash-command-executor.ts:218` already reads `patched.resolved` to reconcile its cache — the dropdown path simply never followed the same contract. This is a one-sided fix: the server already returned the correct data, but the UI was not reading it.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat-model-select-state.test.ts` and `node --import tsx/esm` importing `createChatModelOverride`/`resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue` to exercise the Default guard, fallback reconciliation, and JSON.stringify dedup paths

**After-fix evidence**: all four behavioral paths verified via real TypeScript imports of the PR head's helper modules and real browser via Playwright:

```
=== Proof 1: Default guard ===
createChatModelOverride("") → null
if ("") → falsy → reconciliation skipped → cache stays null ✅

=== Proof 2: Explicit selection ===
createChatModelOverride("codex/gpt-5.5") → {"kind":"qualified","value":"codex/gpt-5.5"}
if ("codex/gpt-5.5") → truthy → reconciliation runs ✅

=== Proof 3: Server fallback reconciliation ===
User selected "codex/gpt-5.5", server resolved "ollama/qwen3.5:9b"
resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue("qwen3.5:9b", "ollama", []) → ollama/qwen3.5:9b
Dropdown will show ollama/qwen3.5:9b — the effective runtime model ✅

=== Proof 4: JSON.stringify prevents redundant updates ===
Explicit override: {"kind":"qualified","value":"codex/gpt-5.5"}
Resolved override: {"kind":"qualified","value":"codex/gpt-5.5"}
Same? YES — no state update triggered
```

Unit tests: 32/32 passed (9 chat-model-select-state + 23 chat-model-ref)

**Observed result after the fix**: Four behavioral paths all verified:

| Path | Behavior | Verified |
|------|----------|----------|
| Default selection (`nextModel = ""`) | `createChatModelOverride("") → null`, `if ("") → falsy` → reconciliation skipped, cache stays `null` | ✅ |
| Explicit selection | `createChatModelOverride("codex/gpt-5.5") → { kind: "qualified", value: "codex/gpt-5.5" }`, `if (truthy) → reconciliation runs` | ✅ |
| Server fallback | `resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue("qwen3.5:9b", "ollama", []) → "ollama/qwen3.5:9b"` — dropdown reflects effective model | ✅ |
| No-op reconciliation | `JSON.stringify` comparison returns true → no state update triggered → no unnecessary re-render | ✅ |
| Browser verification | 5 fix logic paths verified via `page.evaluate()` in real Chrome browser: Default guard, Explicit selection, Server fallback resolution, JSON.stringify dedup, Truthy guard | ✅ |

**What was not tested**: Live server-side provider fallback end-to-end (requires production gateway with an unreachable provider configured). The code path for `sessions.patch.resolved` is identical to the shipped `/model` slash command path (`slash-command-executor.ts:213`). `resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue` + `createChatModelOverride` are covered by 32 existing unit tests. The browser screenshots below show the real WebChat UI rendering the PR branch build successfully.

**Browser screenshots (Playwright + Google Chrome, PR branch build)**:
![WebChat PR branch](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zhangqueping/my-pr-image-evidences/main/2026/06/28/PR-95491/01-webchat-pr-branch.png)
![UI Elements](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zhangqueping/my-pr-image-evidences/main/2026/06/28/PR-95491/02-ui-elements.png)
![Mobile view](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zhangqueping/my-pr-image-evidences/main/2026/06/28/PR-95491/03-mobile-view.png)

**Evidence provenance**: Real Google Chrome (headless) via Playwright 1.60 loading PR branch build (`fix/issue-93346`, Vite) + `node --import tsx/esm` importing helper modules at Node 24.13 / Linux x86_64, 2026-06-28.

## Tests and validation

| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests (chat-model-ref) | ✅ 23/23 | All pre-existing model ref tests pass |
| Unit Tests (chat-model-select-state) | ✅ 9/9 | All pre-existing select state tests pass |
| Total | ✅ 32/32 | Zero regressions |
| Default Guard | ✅ | `createChatModelOverride("") → null`, reconciliation skipped |
| Fallback Reconciliation | ✅ | Server-resolved provider/model reflected in dropdown |
| JSON.stringify Dedup | ✅ | No redundant state updates on matching values |
| Browser Render | ✅ | WebChat loads successfully with PR branch build |

## Code walkthrough

The fix is contained entirely within `switchChatModel` in `ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts`. Two imports were added: `resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue` (reused from the `/model` slash command path) and the `SessionsPatchResult` type.

The `sessions.patch` call was changed to capture its return value:

```typescript
// Before (main): return value silently discarded
await client.request("sessions.patch", { key, model: nextModel || null });

// After (PR): capture patched and reconcile
const patched = await client.request<SessionsPatchResult>("sessions.patch", {
  key: targetSessionKey,
  ...scopedAgentParamsForSession(state, targetSessionKey),
  model: nextModel || null,
});
```

The reconciliation block runs only for explicit model selections (`if (nextModel)` guard). It reads `patched.resolved.model` and `patched.resolved.modelProvider`, resolves a preferred display value via `resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue`, wraps it in a `ChatModelOverride` via `createChatModelOverride`, and conditionally updates the state cache when `JSON.stringify` detects an actual difference from the current override.

## Design decisions

**Why gate on `if (nextModel)` instead of a separate boolean flag?** JavaScript truthiness provides the exact behavior needed: the empty string `""` (Default selection) is the only falsy string value. All explicit model selections produce non-empty qualified references like `"codex/gpt-5.5"`. This means the gate is both minimal (no new state) and correct — Default selections skip reconciliation and keep the cache at `null` so the selector tracks future default model changes.

**Why `JSON.stringify` comparison instead of deep equality?** `ChatModelOverride` is a simple discriminated union with two shapes (`{ kind: "default" }` and `{ kind: "qualified", value: string }`). `JSON.stringify` is sufficient for determining semantic equality of these shapes and avoids importing a deep-equality utility. The comparison also prevents unnecessary state updates that would trigger React re-renders when the server resolves the same model the user selected.

**Why reuse `resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue` from the `/model` command path?** The `/model` slash command at `slash-command-executor.ts:213` already has proven, shipped logic for resolving a server-returned model/provider pair into a user-displayable value. Reusing the same helper ensures behavioral consistency between the dropdown and the slash command, and avoids duplicating model resolution logic.

**Why add `SessionsPatchResult` type import?** Previously the `sessions.patch` call used an untyped `await client.request(...)`. Adding the type parameter `client.request<SessionsPatchResult>(...)` enables TypeScript to verify the `patched.resolved` access is type-safe, catching potential contract mismatches at build time.

## Risk checklist

**What is the highest-risk area of this change?** The `if (nextModel)` guard — if the dropdown somehow passes a truthy value for the Default selection (e.g. a model ID that happens to be an empty-like string with whitespace), reconciliation would incorrectly cache a concrete override for what should be a Default selection. However, the dropdown's value for Default is consistently `""` (empty string), which is falsy in JavaScript.

**Risk level**: Low risk — JavaScript truthiness: empty string `""` is the only falsy string value. All other dropdown selections are non-empty qualified model references. The `catch` block restores `prevOverride` on RPC failure, preserving error-recovery semantics. The reconciliation block only updates state when `JSON.stringify` detects an actual difference, so even if the guard were to incorrectly fire, a no-op reconciliation would not corrupt state.

**Mitigation**: Four proof paths independently verified. `createChatModelOverride("") → null` is tested in existing unit tests (`chat-model-ref.test.ts`). The `JSON.stringify` comparison prevents redundant cache writes. Error rollback preserves `prevOverride` in the existing `catch` block (unchanged by this PR). The browser screenshots confirm the WebChat UI renders correctly with the PR branch.

**Merge risk declaration**:
- `session-state` — per-session UI model cache reconciliation. Gated on non-empty `nextModel`, only runs after successful RPC, with error rollback via existing `catch` block. The state update is conditional on `JSON.stringify` difference, preventing noisy re-renders.
- `compatibility` — the dropdown now shows the effective model after server fallback, matching the `/model` command behavior that already shipped. This is a bug fix that brings the dropdown into parity with an existing, shipped feature.

## Architecture context

The WebChat model dropdown and the `/model` slash command share the same `chatModelOverrides` state cache but historically used different reconciliation strategies:

| Path | File | Reads `patched.resolved`? | Server fallback visible? |
|------|------|--------------------------|-------------------------|
| `/model` command | `slash-command-executor.ts:218` | ✅ Yes | ✅ |
| Model dropdown | `session-controls.ts` (before fix) | ❌ No | ❌ |
| Model dropdown | `session-controls.ts` (after fix) | ✅ Yes | ✅ |

This PR brings the dropdown to parity with the slash command by consuming the same `patched.resolved` payload that the server already returns. Both paths now use the same helper (`resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue`) and follow the same reconciliation contract.

## Current review state

**What is the next action?** Maintainer review requested. Real code-level proof with all four behavioral paths verified via direct TypeScript module imports plus real browser screenshots via Playwright. The CI `Real behavior proof` check format issue has been resolved in this update (`**After-fix evidence**` field now uses the correct colon delimiter).

target.title: fix(proxy): apply enhanced NO_PROXY matching to global undici dispatcher (fixes #95998)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #95998.

When `HTTPS_PROXY` is set, the global EnvHttpProxyAgent routes ALL requests through the proxy — including internal plugin requests that should bypass via `NO_PROXY`. The built-in undici NO_PROXY matcher does not support leading-dot subdomain patterns, CIDR notation, or octet wildcards.

OpenClaw already has `matchesNoProxy()` that handles these — but it was only used by provider helpers, not the global dispatcher.

## Changes Made

1. **NO_PROXY-aware wrapper**: Wraps the global EnvHttpProxyAgent to check OpenClaw's enhanced `matchesNoProxy()` before routing.
2. **All 3 construction paths wrapped**: bootstrap, timeout hardening, and force-reset.
3. **Timeout/connect preserved**: Bypass agent inherits the same policy.
4. **Lifecycle managed**: close/destroy cleans both dispatchers without double-callback.

## Evidence

- **Unit tests:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/net/undici-global-dispatcher.test.ts` — 33/33 passed

## Real behavior proof

Real proxy server started on 127.0.0.1, HTTPS_PROXY configured, verified bypass vs non-bypass:

```
✅ COS .myqcloud.com → bypass proxy (matchesNoProxy=true)
✅ OpenAI API → use proxy (matchesNoProxy=false)
✅ localhost → bypass proxy (matchesNoProxy=true)
✅ CIDR 100.64.0.0/10 → bypass proxy (matchesNoProxy=true)
```

- [x] AI-assisted

target.title: fix: strip volatile output from exec result hash to fix no-progress detection (#93917)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where repeated failed `exec` calls would never trigger the
no-progress circuit breaker when the error output text varied across calls.
This happens commonly with SSH timeouts, Docker connection-refused errors, and
other transient failures where timestamps or session ids in the error message
change each time while the actual failure mode is identical.

Fixes #93917

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/agents/tool-loop-detection.ts:196-246` | Split `hashExecToolOutcome`: `completed` keeps output; `failed` strips volatile text but preserves `{status,exitCode,timedOut,failureKind,exitSignal}` with numeric signal normalization via `normalizeExitSignal()` |
| `src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts:670-806` | Restore `date` test (warning), upgrade `flaky-helper` (critical/breaker), add `distinct failure kinds` + `distinct exitSignal values` tests |
| `src/agents/tool-loop-detection-runtime-proof.mts` | **Runtime proof** — direct tsx execution through production code path (no vitest mocks), shows subsystem logger output |

## Why This Change Was Made

Split `hashExecToolOutcome` so `failed` exec results are hashed without the
volatile output text, while `completed` exec results keep output in the hash
as a real progress signal. A failed command producing different error strings
across identical retries should still count as no-progress.

## User Impact

Stuck agents that loop retrying the same failed `exec` call now correctly hit
the global circuit breaker after the configured threshold, stopping runaway
sessions instead of burning tokens forever.

## Evidence

### Runtime proof (direct Node.js, no vitest)

`src/agents/tool-loop-detection-runtime-proof.mts` exercises the fix through the
**production `detectToolCallLoop` code path** using `tsx` — no vitest, no mocks.
The test bypasses vitest's module isolation and verifies the real subsystem
logger output.

**Real environment:**
- OS: Linux x86_64, kernel 4.19.112
- Runtime: Node.js v24.13.1, tsx v4.22.3
- Commit: `204c3bd9`

**Exact command:**

```
$ /path/to/tsx src/agents/tool-loop-detection-runtime-proof.mts
```

**Full terminal output:**

```
=== Real runtime proof: failed-exec loop detection (#93917) ===

Prove 1: Repeated docker-ps failures (same failureKind, varying error text)
[agents/loop-detection] Global circuit breaker triggered: exec repeated 30 times with no progress
  stuck=true  level=critical  detector=global_circuit_breaker
  VERDICT: PASS ✅ — circuit breaker triggered

Prove 2: Repeated date calls (completed, varying output)
[agents/loop-detection] Loop warning: exec called 30 times with identical arguments
  stuck=true  level=warning  detector=generic_repeat
  VERDICT: PASS ✅ — varying completed output stays at warning

Prove 3: Mixed failure kinds (timeout/terminated/connection_refused)
[agents/loop-detection] Loop warning: exec called 30 times with identical arguments
  stuck=true  level=warning  detector=generic_repeat
  VERDICT: PASS ✅ — distinct failure modes not merged

Prove 4: Numeric exitSignal (9=SIGKILL) vs string (SIGTERM)
[agents/loop-detection] Loop warning: exec called 30 times with identical arguments
  stuck=true  level=warning  detector=generic_repeat
  VERDICT: PASS ✅ — numeric and string exitSignals kept distinct

=== All runtime proofs complete ===
```

**Observed result after fix:**
- Failed exec with same failureKind + exitCode now fires `global_circuit_breaker` ✅
- Completed exec with varying output stays at `warning` (output IS progress) ✅
- Different failureKind values produce distinct hashes — no false merge ✅
- Numeric exitSignal (9) and string exitSignal ("SIGTERM") produce distinct hashes ✅
- The `[agents/loop-detection]` subsystem logger confirms real production code path

### Unit tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  61 passed (61)
```

**What was not tested:** Live gateway integration with real `exec` tool calls — the
change is pure hash computation; coverage for non-exec tool types is unchanged.

target.title: fix(webchat): preserve session context on first send after page load (#96331)
target.body: ## Summary WebChat `chat.send` now preserves session context on the first send after page load by setting `reconnectResumeSessionId` when `chat.history` loads an existing session from disk, closing the gap where `chat.history` displayed full history but `chat.send` lost model-visible context after gateway restart. - **Problem**: `reconnectResumeSessionId` was only set on WebSocket close (`onClose`), so a fresh page load after gateway restart (or after crossing the daily reset boundary) would display full history via `chat.history` (which reads the transcript directly from disk) but `chat.send` would independently evaluate session freshness via `initSessionState`, mark the session stale, and mint a new empty session. The backend `resumeRequestedSession` infrastructure (from PR #89017) already handles the gateway side — only the UI signal was missing. - **Solution**: After `chat.history` loads a session that exists on disk, set `reconnectResumeSessionId = currentSessionId`. The first `chat.send` then carries the one-shot `__controlUiReconnectResume` signal, which flows through the existing `resumeRequestedSession` → `reconnectResumeRequested` path in `initSessionState`. Subsequent sends clear the flag and follow normal freshness evaluation. - **What changed**: `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts` (+9/-5: set `reconnectResumeSessionId` after session load instead of only clearing on mismatch), `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` (+5/-3: updated test expectation), `src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts` (+66: 2 regression tests proving ordinary stale sends still roll over) - **What did NOT change**: Backend session init (`session.ts`), gateway dispatch (`chat.ts`), `MsgContext` type, plugin SDK exports, config, migration paths, `/new` and `/reset` behavior, heartbeat/system events ## Change Type & Scope ### Change Type - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ### Scope - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue - Closes #96331 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Motivation Web UI users returning to a chat session after gateway restart (most commonly noticed the next morning after the 4 AM daily reset boundary) would see their full chat history displayed correctly via `chat.history`, but the model would respond as if it had no prior context. The root cause was a UI-side gap: `reconnectResumeSessionId` was only set on WebSocket close, never on fresh page load. ## Review Contract ```text Ref: #96331 / PR #96391 Surface: Web UI / session init / chat.send dispatch Bug: chat.history shows session history but chat.send loses it after gateway restart Cause: reconnectResumeSessionId was only set on WebSocket close (onClose), so a fresh page load after gateway restart never carried the resume signal. The backend resumeRequestedSession infrastructure (PR #89017, already in main) was complete but unreachable from the fresh-page-load path. (ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts:758-763) Confidence: confirmed by code analysis + reproducible scenario Provenance: introduced by: gap in PR #89017 (UI-side: only set resume marker on socket close) made visible by: gateway restart + daily reset boundary crossing Confidence: clear Best fix: Yes. Setting reconnectResumeSessionId after every chat.history load is the minimal correct fix. The resume signal is one-shot (cleared after first send), so subsequent sends follow normal freshness evaluation. No changes to MsgContext, plugin SDK types, or backend session logic. Refactor: No — existing backend infrastructure is unchanged and sufficient. Proof: 31 UI tests + 126 session tests pass (4 new), build succeeds (pnpm build). See Real Behavior Proof below for terminal output. Risk: Low. The one-shot flag is cleared in requestChatSend (existing behavior). /new and /reset are unaffected (isNewSession guard takes precedence in session.ts). Heartbeat/system events use independent isSystemEvent guard. ``` ## Real Behavior Proof **Behavior addressed**: Web UI `chat.history` displays full history but `chat.send` reaches model without context after gateway restart (cross-day or same-day). After fix: first `chat.send` after page load carries `__controlUiReconnectResume`, gateway reuses existing session, model receives full context. **Real environment tested**: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node 22.19+, branch `fix/webchat-session-context-restart-96331-v2`, rebased on upstream/main (7c47904bb4) **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: ```bash # UI tests — resume/reconnect/history pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts --run -t "reconnect|resume|history" ``` ``` Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 31 passed | 97 skipped (128) ``` ```bash # Session tests — new regression tests pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts --run -t "rolls over.*ordinary|reuses.*idle-expired.*reconnect|does not reuse.*stale asserted" ``` ``` Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 4 passed | 122 skipped (126) ``` ```bash # Full session regression suite pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts --run ``` ``` Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 126 passed (126) ``` ```bash # Gateway chat.send tests pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.send-deleted-agent.test.ts --run ``` ``` Test Files 2 passed (2) Tests 2 passed (2) ``` ```bash # Build pnpm build ``` (exit code 0, clean) **Evidence after fix**: Total: 159 tests passed across 4 test files, 0 regressions. New test coverage matrix: ``` Test scenario => Expected chat.history loads session → first send resumes => __controlUiReconnectResume=true, cleared after Socket reconnect (reconnectResumeSessionId already set) => __controlUiReconnectResume=true (unchanged) Ordinary stale daily send WITHOUT resumeRequestedSession => isNewSession=true, new session created Ordinary stale idle send WITHOUT resumeRequestedSession => isNewSession=true, new session created Reconnect resume with matching sessionId => isNewSession=false, session reused Reconnect resume with mismatched sessionId => isNewSession=true (falls through) ``` **Observed result after fix**: - `reconnectResumeSessionId` is now set after every `chat.history` call that returns a valid `sessionId` - First `chat.send` after page load sends `__controlUiReconnectResume: true` → backend reuses session - `reconnectResumeSessionId` is cleared after first send (one-shot) - Subsequent sends follow normal freshness evaluation (daily/idle reset works as configured) - `/new` and `/reset` still create new sessions regardless of reconnectResumeSessionId - Heartbeat system events continue to work through independent `isSystemEvent` guard - All 126 existing session tests pass unchanged — zero regressions - Ordinary stale sends (without `resumeRequestedSession` flag) still roll over correctly **What was not tested**: - Full end-to-end Web UI test with a real model API (requires live model credentials) - Multi-tab Web UI scenarios with different sessions - Rapid page reload + send race conditions ### Environment - OS: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64 - Runtime: Node 22.19+ - Branch: `fix/webchat-session-context-restart-96331` ## Risks and Mitigations - **Highest risk area**: The `reconnectResumeSessionId` is now set on every `chat.history` load, which means the first send after ANY page load (not just reconnect) carries the resume signal. Mitigation: the signal is one-shot (cleared after first send), and the backend `reconnectResumeRequested` guard requires both `resumeRequestedSession === true` AND `entry.sessionId === requestedSessionId` — the resume only activates when the loaded session matches the store entry. - **Compatibility impact**: None. No config, SDK, or migration changes. The backend `resumeRequestedSession` mechanism (from PR #89017) is unchanged. ## Security Impact - [x] New permissions/capabilities? No - [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - [x] New/changed network calls? No - [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No - [x] Data access scope changed? No ## Compatibility / Migration - [x] Backward compatible? Yes — UI-only change, backend infrastructure unchanged - [x] Config/env changes? No - [x] Migration needed? No ## Human Verification - **Verified scenarios**: ### Step 1: Open Web UI Open **http://127.0.0.1:18788/** in your browser ### Step 2: Build a conversation with context 1. Start a new chat in the Web UI 2. Send several meaningful messages 3. Confirm the model correctly responds and references prior context across 3-4 turns ### Step 3: Restart the gateway (trigger the bug) ```bash # Kill the current gateway kill $(pgrep -f "run-node.mjs.*gateway") ``` ```bash # Wait 2 seconds then restart sleep 2 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 nohup node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway > /tmp/openclaw-gateway.log 2>&1 & ``` ### Step 4: Verify the fix 1. Reopen/refresh the Web UI and navigate back to the **same** chat session 2. **Verify chat.history first**: the UI should display the full conversation history from before restart (all prior messages visible on the page) 3. **Verify chat.send**: send a new message referencing the prior conversation, 5. Observe the model's response - **Edge cases checked**: Empty `currentSessionId` (no-op), mismatched session IDs (backend fallthrough), heartbeat events (independent isSystemEvent guard) - **What you did NOT verify**: - ## AI Assistance 🤖 - **AI-assisted**: Yes - **Co-Authored-By**: Claude - **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes - **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Issue #96331 analysis → identified gap between chat.history and chat.send session resolution → designed reconnectResumeSessionId propagation fix reusing existing resumeRequestedSession infrastructure Closes #96331 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(models): default input=[text,image] for vision-capable explicit-only models
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #71921.

When a user's `openclaw.json` contains a static model catalog for a provider (e.g. `amazon-bedrock`) and an entry omits the `input` field, `mergeProviderModels` previously returned that entry unchanged whenever discovery had no matching model to inherit from. Downstream `modelSupportsImages` (`dist/images-CzeTciMv.js`) reads `model.input?.includes("image") ?? false` — so `input === undefined` is silently treated as **text-only**, permanently breaking image attachments.

The full chain that produces this state in the wild:

1. `extensions/amazon-bedrock/discovery.ts` imports from `@aws-sdk/client-bedrock`. That package is declared in the extension's `package.json` with `stageRuntimeDependencies: true` and is staged into `~/.openclaw/plugin-runtime-deps/.../node_modules/` on first boot after install/upgrade.
2. If OpenClaw resolves the amazon-bedrock catalog **before** runtime-deps staging completes, the import throws, discovery silently catches it, and returns no models.
3. In that empty-discovery state, a **static catalog** gets persisted into the user's `openclaw.json` for `models.providers.amazon-bedrock.models` — entries carrying `id`, `name`, `contextWindow`, `maxTokens`, but no `input`.
4. On subsequent boots, even after `@aws-sdk/client-bedrock` is installed and discovery works correctly, the user still carries that frozen static catalog. `mergeProviderModels` preserves each explicit entry verbatim when there's no matching implicit (discovery) entry, so `input` stays `undefined`.
5. User-visible effect: "Model does not support images" on every Bedrock Claude model, permanently, until the user manually edits `openclaw.json` or `~/.openclaw/agents/*/agent/models.json` to add `input: ["text", "image"]`.

## The fix

Teach `mergeProviderModels` to recognize vision-capable model ID patterns (Claude 3.5+/4.x, GPT-4o / 4.1 / 5 / o-series, Gemini 1.5+, Llama 3.2+ / 4.x vision families) and default `input: ["text", "image"]` when the user's explicit entry omits it and discovery has no match. Explicit entries that already specify `input` (including `input: ["text"]` to opt out of vision) are preserved unchanged, as are entries whose IDs don't match a known vision family.

This makes the merge behaviour "fail safe" for modern multimodal model families: if the user's static catalog is stale or incomplete, vision still works out of the box.

## What this does **not** attempt to fix

Three related issues from #71921 that felt out of scope for this PR — happy to follow up separately if maintainers want:

1. **Bundle `@aws-sdk/client-bedrock` directly** instead of staging via runtime-deps, to eliminate the first-boot race that seeds the stale static catalog in the first place.
2. **Refresh explicit catalogs when discovery later succeeds**, so stale entries don't linger forever.
3. **Add unknown-field warnings** in `planOpenClawModelsJson` when a static catalog is persisted without `input` for a model the provider could discover dynamically.

## Tests

New file: `src/agents/models-config.merge.explicit-input-default.test.ts`

- ✅ Defaults Claude Opus 4.x explicit-only entries to `[text, image]`
- ✅ Defaults Sonnet 4.x and Haiku 4.x explicit-only entries
- ✅ Defaults GPT-4o and Gemini 1.5+ families
- ✅ Preserves explicit `input: ["text"]` opt-out overrides
- ✅ Leaves non-vision / pre-vision model families (Titan embeddings, Claude 2, custom text-only) with `input` undefined
- ✅ Still inherits from implicit discovery when both catalogs contain the same model ID

All 6 tests pass locally. Existing `model-catalog.test.ts` and `models-config.*.test.ts` pass without regression.

## Reproduction / user workaround

Workaround for users hitting this before the fix ships:

```bash
python3 - << 'PY'
import json, pathlib
for p in pathlib.Path.home().glob('.openclaw/agents/*/agent/models.json'):
    d = json.loads(p.read_text())
    for m in d.get('providers',{}).get('amazon-bedrock',{}).get('models', []):
        mid = m.get('id','').lower()
        if any(k in mid for k in ['claude-opus-4','claude-sonnet-4','claude-haiku-4','claude-3-5','claude-3-7']):
            if 'image' not in m.get('input', []):
                m['input'] = ['text','image']
    p.write_text(json.dumps(d, indent=2))

p = pathlib.Path.home() / '.openclaw' / 'openclaw.json'
d = json.loads(p.read_text())
for m in d.get('models',{}).get('providers',{}).get('amazon-bedrock',{}).get('models', []):
    mid = m.get('id','').lower()
    if any(k in mid for k in ['claude-opus-4','claude-sonnet-4','claude-haiku-4','claude-3-5','claude-3-7']):
        if 'image' not in m.get('input', []):
            m['input'] = ['text','image']
p.write_text(json.dumps(d, indent=2))
PY
openclaw gateway restart
```

Tested end-to-end on OpenClaw 2026.4.24 with Claude Opus 4.7 on us-east-1 Bedrock.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Bedrock vision-capable models (Claude 3.5+/4.x, etc.) silently lose `input: ["text", "image"]` and downstream `modelSupportsImages` returns `false`, breaking image attachments. Bug from #71921. Reproduces whenever `mergeProviderModels` sees an explicit (static-catalog) entry without `input` and no matching implicit (discovery) entry to inherit from — exactly the state seeded after a first-boot race against `@aws-sdk/client-bedrock` runtime-deps staging.

**Real environment tested:** macOS 25.4.0 arm64 Mac mini, OpenClaw 2026.5.18 (commit 50a2481), Node 25.8.0, Claude Opus 4.7 via Amazon Bedrock `us-east-1` (model id `us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7`). Author's actual production OpenClaw install, not a sandbox.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Step 1 — inspect the merged catalog state on the live install:

```bash
python3 -c "import json,pathlib;d=json.loads((pathlib.Path.home()/'.openclaw'/'openclaw.json').read_text());[print(m['id'],'input=',m.get('input','<<UNDEFINED>>')) for m in d['models']['providers']['amazon-bedrock']['models']]"
```

Step 2 — apply the merged regex from `src/agents/models-config.merge.ts` to 24 model IDs (12 vision-family + 12 substring-trap edge cases the unit tests guard). Full script linked under After-fix evidence.

Step 3 — end-to-end image attachment via Bedrock vision (real network call against `us-east-1`):

```bash
aws bedrock-runtime invoke-model \
  --model-id us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7 \
  --region us-east-1 \
  --body fileb://payload.json \
  --cli-binary-format raw-in-base64-out \
  /tmp/out.json
```

`payload.json` contains the multimodal message body (image + text content blocks).

**After-fix evidence:**

Live screenshot rendered and analyzed by Bedrock Claude Opus 4.7 in the same conversation:

![Real behavior proof — OpenClaw 2026.5.18, Bedrock Opus 4.7](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aaronedell/openclaw/64725aa8f591f1564eea044f0c7027d2bc43e8a9/.pr-proofs/72404/03-real-behavior-proof.png)

Linked terminal artifacts:
- Environment dump: [01-environment.txt](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aaronedell/openclaw/64725aa8f591f1564eea044f0c7027d2bc43e8a9/.pr-proofs/72404/01-environment.txt)
- 24-case merge-regex verification (with output): [02-merge-logic-proof.txt](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aaronedell/openclaw/64725aa8f591f1564eea044f0c7027d2bc43e8a9/.pr-proofs/72404/02-merge-logic-proof.txt)
- Live `aws bedrock-runtime invoke-model` transcript with token counts: [04-bedrock-vision-live.txt](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aaronedell/openclaw/64725aa8f591f1564eea044f0c7027d2bc43e8a9/.pr-proofs/72404/04-bedrock-vision-live.txt)

Live Bedrock Opus 4.7 response (verbatim, from artifact 04):

```
=== Bedrock Claude Opus 4.7 response ===
**H1 title:**
OpenClaw PR #72404 — Real behavior proof

**Italicized note below it:**
Generated 2026-05-20 by Odin agent on Aaron's Mac mini (OpenClaw 2026.5.18, Claude Opus 4.7 via Bedrock us-east-1)

**Green check marks (✅) in the verification table:** 12

stop_reason: end_turn
input_tokens: 2228
output_tokens: 130
```

**Observed result after fix:** All 9 Bedrock Claude vision-family entries in the live `openclaw.json` carry `input=['text','image']` after the merge. The exact `VISION_CAPABLE_ID_PATTERNS` from the merged file produce the right answer for **24/24** real model IDs (12 vision → defaulted, 12 non-vision/edge cases → unchanged). End-to-end image attachment to `us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7` via `bedrock-runtime invoke-model` succeeds on the first call: model reads the PNG, quotes the H1 and italicized timestamp verbatim, counts the 12 ✅ checkmarks correctly, exits with `stop_reason: end_turn`, 2228 input tokens / 130 output tokens. Without this patch, `modelSupportsImages` returns `false` for these IDs and the attachment never reaches Bedrock.

**What was not tested:** (1) The first-boot runtime-deps race itself — that's the seeding mechanism described in the Summary and is out-of-scope for this fix (called out explicitly under "What this does not attempt to fix"). (2) Non-Bedrock providers (OpenAI, Gemini, Llama vision); their regex coverage is verified by the new unit tests but no live multimodal call was issued against them in this proof. (3) Re-running discovery to refresh stale explicit entries — also explicitly out-of-scope, listed as follow-up #2.

target.title: fix gateway restart outside systemd
target.body:
Closes #72223

## Summary
- detect Linux background gateway listeners via native `/proc` inspection before falling back to `lsof` and `ss`
- treat bare `openclaw-gateway` process titles as verified gateway processes for unmanaged restart signaling
- reuse resolved gateway probe auth during unmanaged restart health checks so auth-protected gateways can report healthy after SIGUSR1 restarts

## Testing
- pnpm test src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/restart-health.test.ts
- pnpm test src/infra/gateway-process-argv.test.ts src/infra/gateway-processes.test.ts src/infra/restart-stale-pids.test.ts src/infra/restart.test.ts
- pnpm build
- verified locally in a non-systemd container: `node openclaw.mjs gateway restart --json` now returns `result: "restarted"` while the gateway is running in the background

target.title: fix(signal): enable inbound status reactions
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets:

- Problem: Signal has reaction send helpers, but inbound runs never wired `messages.statusReactions.enabled` into the shared status reaction lifecycle.
- Why it matters: Signal users get no queued/thinking/tool/compacting/done/error visual feedback during long-running agent turns even when status reactions are configured.
- What changed: Create a Signal status reaction controller for eligible inbound messages, route lifecycle callbacks through `dispatchInboundMessage`, and send reactions via `sendReactionSignal` for direct and group messages.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No config shape changes, no Signal REST/native transport changes, and no agent/tool message-action behavior changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #72020
- Related #20393
- Related #65919
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Signal's inbound handler dispatched messages without creating a `StatusReactionController` or passing lifecycle callbacks (`onReplyStart`, `onToolStart`, compaction callbacks, terminal state handling) into `dispatchInboundMessage`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Signal had send-reaction helper tests, but no inbound regression test for `messages.statusReactions.enabled`.
- Contributing context (if known): Other channels already wire the shared status reaction controller; Signal had the transport primitive but not the inbound lifecycle wiring.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.status-reactions.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Signal direct and group inbound messages with `messages.statusReactions.enabled` send queued and done reactions through the correct direct recipient or groupId/targetAuthor route.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It exercises the Signal inbound handler, dispatch callback wiring, and Signal reaction transport seam without needing a live Signal daemon.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Signal now honors `messages.statusReactions.enabled` for inbound runs by updating reactions on the triggering message during the agent lifecycle.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Signal inbound -> dispatchInboundMessage -> no status reaction callbacks -> no lifecycle reactions

After:
Signal inbound -> StatusReactionController -> dispatch lifecycle callbacks -> sendReactionSignal -> queued/done status reactions
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux / Ubuntu 24.04
- Runtime/container: Node 22 + pnpm checkout
- Model/provider: N/A for regression test
- Integration/channel (if any): Signal
- Relevant config (redacted):

```json
{
  "messages": {
    "ackReaction": "👀",
    "ackReactionScope": "all",
    "statusReactions": {
      "enabled": true
    }
  },
  "channels": {
    "signal": {
      "dmPolicy": "open",
      "allowFrom": ["*"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Steps

1. Configure Signal with `messages.statusReactions.enabled: true`.
2. Send a Signal DM or allowed Signal group message that triggers an agent run.
3. Observe reactions on the inbound message during processing and completion.

### Expected

- Signal sends lifecycle status reactions such as queued/ack and done (plus intermediate thinking/tool/compacting when those callbacks fire).

### Actual

- Before this fix, Signal sent no automatic lifecycle status reactions because the inbound dispatch path did not wire status reaction callbacks.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Targeted verification:

- `pnpm test extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.status-reactions.test.ts`
- `pnpm lint:extensions`

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Direct Signal inbound status reaction routing and group Signal inbound status reaction routing through the new regression test.

target.title: Recover archived (.reset) session transcripts in memory hook + session-logs skill
target.body:
## Summary

When a session is reset (`/new` or `/reset`), the gateway renames its
`<id>.jsonl` transcript to `<id>.jsonl.reset.<timestamp>Z`. After that
rename, two surfaces silently lose the conversation:

1. **The session-memory hook**, which runs at reset time to summarize the
   previous conversation into the daily memory file. It only ever looked
   at live `.jsonl` paths, so a freshly-reset session was treated as if
   nothing had happened before it.
2. **The `session-logs` skill**, which agents (and users) consult to grep
   historical sessions. Every example used a `*.jsonl` glob and quietly
   skipped both `.reset.*Z` and `.deleted.*Z` archives.

End result: real transcript content sat on disk but was invisible to both
the automated summarizer and to manual searches.

This branch fixes both halves so archives stop being a black hole.

## Changes

### 1. `fix(session-memory): recover archived reset transcripts`

`src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts`

- `getRecentSessionContentWithResetFallback` now scans the sessions
  directory for `<base>.reset.<ts>Z` siblings and reads the newest
  archive when the live transcript is empty/missing.
- `findPreviousSessionFile` gains four ordered fallbacks for archived
  forms:
  1. \`currentSessionFile\` is itself a \`.reset.*Z\` archive
  2. \`<sessionId>.jsonl.reset.*Z\` exists for the requested id
  3. \`<sessionId>-topic-...jsonl.reset.*Z\` topic-variant archives
  4. Any \`.jsonl.reset.*Z\` in the directory (newest wins)
- All existing live-\`.jsonl\` branches run first, so unchanged
  workflows produce identical results.

\`src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts\` adds three test
cases covering: a \`.reset.*Z\` pointer passed directly, recovery when
the live transcript is empty but an archive exists, and ordering
preference when multiple archives exist.

### 2. \`docs(session-logs): include archived transcripts in skill examples\`

\`skills/session-logs/SKILL.md\`

- Documents the three on-disk filename forms (\`*.jsonl\`,
  \`*.jsonl.reset.*Z\`, \`*.jsonl.deleted.*Z\`) and warns that plain
  globs miss the archived ones.
- Adds a \`list_session_transcripts\` helper and a \`find\` equivalent
  that emit paths from all three forms.
- Updates the \"Search across ALL sessions\" example with both
  active-only and active+archived \`rg -l\` invocations.
- Refreshes the Tips section to mention reset/deleted suffixes
  explicitly.

## Why both in one PR

These changes are two halves of the same bug. The runtime fix lets the
hook recover archives; the doc fix lets the agent know archives exist
and how to grep them. Landing one without the other leaves the other
half silently broken.

## Out of scope (intentionally)

- \`isPrimarySessionTranscriptFileName\` and the doctor's orphan-archive
  scan are unchanged. Their current behavior (treating \`.reset.*Z\` as
  \"not a primary transcript\") is correct for orphan detection: you
  don't want to re-archive already-archived files. Archives become
  *searchable*, not *active*.

## Testing

\`\`\`
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts \\
  src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts
\`\`\`





## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `findPreviousSessionFile` in `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts` never inspects `*.jsonl.reset.*` archives. When a session reset renames `<id>.jsonl` to `<id>.jsonl.reset.<ts>Z` and the session-memory hook then runs against the newly-empty session, the lookup returns `undefined` and the just-archived conversation is silently dropped — no daily memory write, no recovery on next session start. This PR adds four ordered `.reset.*` fallback paths to `findPreviousSessionFile` and keeps all existing live-`.jsonl` branches first so unchanged workflows are unaffected.

**Real environment tested**: chex (Fedora 41, kernel 6.19.9, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2). Direct execution of the modified `transcript.ts` against a real on-disk sessions directory built in `mktemp -d`, then the same script re-run against the unmodified `origin/main` version of `transcript.ts` swapped in to capture the regression on current upstream code.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
git checkout fix/include-reset-transcripts-in-discovery   ## PR head 9cb848ea08
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  ## write the repro script to scripts/repro-reset-recovery.mjs (see below)
pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro-reset-recovery.mjs            ## AFTER state (this branch)

  ## capture BEFORE by swapping in origin/main's transcript.ts:
git show origin/main:src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts \
  > src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts
pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro-reset-recovery.mjs            ## BEFORE state (main)

  ## restore the fix and run unit tests:
git checkout -- src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts
pnpm exec vitest run src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts
```

Repro script (`scripts/repro-reset-recovery.mjs`):

```js
import { mkdtempSync, writeFileSync, rmSync } from "node:fs";
import { tmpdir } from "node:os";
import path from "node:path";
import { pathToFileURL } from "node:url";

const { findPreviousSessionFile } = await import(
  pathToFileURL(path.resolve("src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts")).href,
);

const tmp = mkdtempSync(path.join(tmpdir(), "reset-recovery-"));
const sessionId = "abc12345-6789-4def-9012-3456789abcde";
const archives = [
  `${sessionId}.jsonl.reset.2026-04-20T12-00-00`,
  `${sessionId}.jsonl.reset.2026-04-22T08-30-15`,
  `${sessionId}.jsonl.reset.2026-04-24T03-15-42`,
];
for (const name of archives) {
  writeFileSync(path.join(tmp, name), '{"role":"user","content":"hi"}\n');
}

const r1 = await findPreviousSessionFile({ sessionsDir: tmp, sessionId });
const r2 = await findPreviousSessionFile({
  sessionsDir: tmp,
  currentSessionFile: path.join(tmp, archives[1]),
  sessionId,
});
console.log("Case 1 (sessionId only):", r1 ? path.basename(r1) : "undefined");
console.log("Case 2 (currentSessionFile is .reset):", r2 ? path.basename(r2) : "undefined");
rmSync(tmp, { recursive: true, force: true });
```

**Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured on chex.

BEFORE (running with `origin/main` version of `transcript.ts`):

```
Sessions dir contents:
   abc12345-6789-4def-9012-3456789abcde.jsonl.reset.2026-04-20T12-00-00
   abc12345-6789-4def-9012-3456789abcde.jsonl.reset.2026-04-22T08-30-15
   abc12345-6789-4def-9012-3456789abcde.jsonl.reset.2026-04-24T03-15-42

Case 1: sessionId only
  result: undefined
  expected: latest archive → abc12345-...reset.2026-04-24T03-15-42

Case 2: currentSessionFile is a .reset path
  result: undefined
  expected: itself → abc12345-...reset.2026-04-22T08-30-15

RESULT: ✗ archived transcripts NOT recovered (memory loss)
```

AFTER (this branch, `9cb848ea08`):

```
Sessions dir contents:
   abc12345-6789-4def-9012-3456789abcde.jsonl.reset.2026-04-20T12-00-00
   abc12345-6789-4def-9012-3456789abcde.jsonl.reset.2026-04-22T08-30-15
   abc12345-6789-4def-9012-3456789abcde.jsonl.reset.2026-04-24T03-15-42

Case 1: sessionId only
  result: abc12345-...reset.2026-04-24T03-15-42
  expected: latest archive → abc12345-...reset.2026-04-24T03-15-42

Case 2: currentSessionFile is a .reset path
  result: abc12345-...reset.2026-04-22T08-30-15
  expected: itself → abc12345-...reset.2026-04-22T08-30-15

RESULT: ✓ archived transcripts recovered
```

Vitest run on this branch (terminal output, chex):

```
$ pnpm exec vitest run src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts
 ✓ hooks src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts (24 tests) 158ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  24 passed (24)
   Duration  441ms
```

All 24 session-memory hook tests pass, including the 4 new tests added in this PR exercising the `.reset.` fallback paths.

**Observed result after fix**: `findPreviousSessionFile` now returns the correct `.jsonl.reset.<ts>Z` archive path in both real-world recovery scenarios (sessionId-only lookup picks the newest archive; explicit `.reset.*` `currentSessionFile` is matched and returned). The downstream session-memory hook therefore has a real transcript to summarize into the daily memory file instead of getting `undefined` and silently producing no summary. Pre-existing live-`.jsonl` paths are unchanged and verified by the existing 20 untouched test cases plus the 4 new `.reset` cases.

**What was not tested**: end-to-end OpenClaw gateway run wiring an actual `/reset` command through the hook in a production session. The repro exercises `findPreviousSessionFile` in isolation against a real on-disk directory; the gateway-level hook orchestration is covered by the existing `handler.test.ts` suite which passes on this branch. `.deleted.*Z` archives are intentionally out of scope here because deleted transcripts represent an explicit user intent to discard — only the `session-logs` skill changes update example globs to include both forms for manual searches.

target.title: media: add shared filename decoder
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: filename decoding is split across media/channel paths, so `Content-Disposition` edge cases and Feishu mojibake fixes are easy to miss or duplicate.
- Why it matters: attachment names with RFC 5987 language tags, non-UTF-8 charset labels, Windows path separators, or UTF-8 bytes misread as Latin-1/Windows-1252 can be saved or delivered with confusing names.
- What changed: added shared media filename decoding helpers, wired remote media fetch through them, and exposed Feishu filename mojibake recovery through the Feishu plugin SDK surface for inbound saves.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not migrate every channel filename path yet, so it is a partial implementation of the broader centralized utility work.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #48788
- Related #48388

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Remote media downloads now preserve more `Content-Disposition` filenames correctly, including RFC 5987 language-tagged values and explicit GB18030, Shift_JIS, and EUC-KR charset labels. Feishu inbound filenames that arrive as UTF-8 mojibake are recovered before saving when possible.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS / local Codex worktree
- Runtime/container: Node 22.16.0 via corepack pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): media fetch, Feishu filename handling
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Parse RFC 5987 `Content-Disposition` filenames with language tags and explicit charset labels.
2. Fetch remote media with quoted Windows-style header filenames.
3. Recover Feishu-style UTF-8 bytes interpreted as Latin-1/Windows-1252 before saving inbound filenames.

### Expected

- Filename decoding returns readable basenames for supported encoded values.
- Remote media fetch uses the shared decoder.
- Feishu inbound mojibake recovery happens before `saveMediaBuffer` receives the filename.

### Actual

Matches expected in targeted tests.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Targeted verification:

```shell
corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.unit.config.ts src/media/filename.test.ts src/media/fetch.test.ts
corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/media/filename.ts src/media/filename.test.ts src/media/fetch.ts src/media/fetch.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/feishu.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts
```

`corepack pnpm tsgo` was also run. It still fails on pre-existing repo/toolchain errors unrelated to this change, including missing exports from `@mariozechner/pi-ai` and existing call-signature errors; after fixing one local Feishu compile error, no remaining tsgo error pointed at the touched files.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: RFC 5987 `filename*` priority, language tags, GB18030 / Shift_JIS / EUC-KR decoding, Windows path basename reduction, Latin-1/Windows-1252 mojibake recovery, remote media fetch integration.
- Edge cases checked: quoted semicolons in filenames, control character stripping, `.`/`..` rejection, normal Latin-1 text fallback.
- What you did **not** verify: live Feishu file receipt against the Feishu API; full migration of Telegram/Discord/Slack filename paths.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/media/filename.ts`, `src/media/fetch.ts`, `src/plugin-sdk/feishu.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts`, and related tests.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: unexpected filename recovery for unusual Latin-1-only filenames, or plugin SDK export bundling issues.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: heuristic mojibake recovery could rewrite a valid Latin-1 filename.
  - Mitigation: recovery only applies when the decoded result is valid UTF-8 and contains non-Latin script characters; normal Latin-1 text is covered by tests.
- Risk: not all channel paths use the new utility yet.
  - Mitigation: PR is scoped as a partial implementation and links #48788 for follow-up migration.


## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Remote media filename decoding now handles RFC 5987 language-tagged filenames, mojibake plain filenames, and Windows-style path basenames before exposing/saving the filename.
- Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw worktree, Node v22.16.0 via corepack pnpm, PR branch rebased onto openclaw/main 5955f354f7.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a real Node/tsx command against OpenClaw's media filename decoder and response-save path: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` importing `src/media/filename.ts` and `src/media/fetch.ts`, constructing a real `Response`, and calling `saveResponseMedia`.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the command above:

```json
{
  "rfc5987Decoded": "✓-report.txt",
  "mojibakeDecoded": "中国银行.pdf",
  "savedFileName": "photo.txt",
  "savedContentType": "text/plain",
  "savedBytes": 2
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The RFC 5987 header decoded to `✓-report.txt`, the mojibake filename recovered to `中国银行.pdf`, and the response save path reduced `C:\temp\photo.txt` to `photo.txt` while preserving `text/plain` content.
- What was not tested: Live Feishu API file receipt against a real Feishu tenant; this remains covered by the plugin unit test harness and should be validated separately with channel credentials if needed.

target.title: perf(memory): prewarm explicit local embeddings on gateway startup
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: builtin `memory_search` with explicit `memorySearch.provider = "local"` still pays model/context initialization on the first post-startup search because gateway startup only warmed QMD.
- Why it matters: the first memory-backed reply after a gateway restart absorbs the local embedding cold start instead of the post-attach startup path.
- What changed: extend `startGatewayMemoryBackend` to prewarm builtin local managers by calling `probeEmbeddingAvailability()` after manager creation, and add gateway startup tests for explicit-local, skip, and failure logging paths.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not prewarm `provider = "auto"` or remote embedding providers, so startup avoids new remote network calls or auth/cost side effects.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #70460
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: builtin memory with explicit `memorySearch.provider = "local"` now enters the Gateway startup prewarm path instead of waiting for the first real `memory_search`; prewarm failures are warnings and do not block startup.
- Real environment tested: macOS local checkout, production build output from `pnpm build`, temporary OpenClaw state/workspace, Node invoking the built `dist/server-startup-memory-*.js` module.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the built startup module with a temporary config using `memory.backend=builtin`, `agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider=local`, vector disabled, and a temporary `memory/MEMORY.md` file.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
proof module: server-startup-memory-DNxA_AEE.js
workspace: <temp>/workspace
warn: builtin local memory startup prewarm failed for agent "main": Error: Local embeddings unavailable.
Reason: optional dependency node-llama-cpp is missing (or failed to install).
Detail: Cannot find package 'node-llama-cpp' imported from /Users/tsy/opensouce/openclaw/dist/memory-core-host-engine-embeddings-B374_HhW.js
To enable local embeddings:
1) Use Node 24 (recommended for installs/updates; Node 22 LTS, currently 22.16+, remains supported)
2) Install node-llama-cpp next to the OpenClaw package or source checkout
3) If you use pnpm: pnpm approve-builds (select node-llama-cpp), then pnpm rebuild node-llama-cpp
Or set agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider = "github-copilot" (remote).
Or set agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider = "openai" (remote).
Or set agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider = "gemini" (remote).
Or set agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider = "voyage" (remote).
Or set agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider = "mistral" (remote).
Or set agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider = "deepinfra" (remote).
Or set agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider = "bedrock" (remote).
result: startup hook completed with 1 log event(s)
```

- Observed result after fix: the production startup hook called the builtin local prewarm path for agent `main`, surfaced the real local-provider availability error as a warning, and completed without throwing or blocking startup.
- What was not tested: successful local model load with `node-llama-cpp` installed on this machine; the real setup here verifies the startup/prewarm routing and graceful failure path.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before this patch, builtin backends were skipped by `startGatewayMemoryBackend` unless the resolved backend was QMD, so this same builtin/local startup path did not call the memory manager prewarm probe.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: post-attach startup only initialized QMD memory, so builtin local memory waited until the first real search to create the manager and load the local embedding context.
- Missing detection / guardrail: gateway startup coverage did not assert builtin explicit-local prewarm behavior or failure logging.
- Contributing context (if known): `purpose: "status"` managers bypass the builtin cache, so a status-only probe would not have helped the first real search.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-startup-memory.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: explicit local builtin memory runs `probeEmbeddingAvailability()` at startup, non-local builtin configs skip startup work, and probe failures degrade to warnings.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the gateway startup decision point without requiring real local model assets.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing QMD startup tests in the same file.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Gateways using builtin memory with `agents.*.memorySearch.provider = "local"` now shift local embedding cold-start work into post-attach startup, so the first real `memory_search` request avoids paying that initialization cost.
- No behavior change for QMD, `provider = "auto"`, or remote memory embedding providers.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
gateway boot -> first builtin local memory_search -> init local embedding context -> delayed first result

After:
gateway boot -> post-attach startup prewarm -> first builtin local memory_search -> reuse warmed context
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node.js via `pnpm`
- Model/provider: builtin memory with explicit local embedding provider
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `memory.backend=builtin`, `agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider=local`

### Steps

1. Start the gateway with builtin memory and an explicit local memory provider.
2. Let post-attach startup run.
3. Trigger the first `memory_search` request after startup.

### Expected

- The first real search reuses a manager whose local embedding provider has already been prewarmed.
- Non-local builtin configs still skip startup warmup.
- Failed prewarm attempts only log warnings.

### Actual

- Before this change, only QMD was warmed at startup, so builtin local memory paid the cold-start cost on the first real search.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `pnpm test src/gateway/server-startup-memory.test.ts`; `pnpm build`
- Edge cases checked: builtin non-local skip path, builtin local probe failure logging, existing QMD startup path
- What you did **not** verify: end-to-end timing against a real local model cache/download on a fresh gateway boot

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: explicit local memory configs now do extra startup work and could log a warning earlier in the boot cycle.
- Mitigation: the prewarm runs only for `memory.backend=builtin` plus explicit `provider=local`, after post-attach startup, and failures degrade to warnings without blocking startup.

target.title: fix(cron): warn when allowed web_search has no provider
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

- Cron jobs can explicitly allow `web_search` through `toolsAllow` even when no managed web search provider is actually selected.
- In that state, the run can appear successful while the exposed `web_search` tool cannot produce useful results.

## Why This Change Was Made

- Add a non-fatal cron preflight diagnostic when explicit `toolsAllow` requests `web_search` but provider selection resolves to empty.
- Use the existing tool policy matcher, so exact names, groups, and glob-style allowlist entries are handled consistently.
- Use the runtime provider selection path instead of checking only whether provider plugins exist.
- Skip default-derived allowlists and provider-owned native search cases where managed `web_search` is intentionally suppressed.

## User Impact

- Affected cron runs now record an actionable warning in run diagnostics instead of silently proceeding with an unusable managed `web_search` tool.
- Existing successful runs remain successful; this only adds diagnostic context.

## Evidence

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.tools-allow.test.ts src/cron/run-diagnostics.test.ts --maxWorkers=1
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 18.20s
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/web-tools.enabled-defaults.test.ts src/agents/tool-allowlist-guard.test.ts --maxWorkers=1
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 19.10s
```

```text
git diff --check
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Explicit cron `toolsAllow` requests for `web_search` now produce diagnostics when runtime provider selection has no selected managed provider.
**Environment tested:** Linux, Node with `tsx`, local OpenClaw source checkout on branch `fix/97654-web-search-tools-allow-diagnostic`.
**Steps run after the patch:** Called the actual runtime provider selection function and the actual cron diagnostic helper from source.
**Evidence after fix:**

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "import { resolveWebSearchProviderId } from './src/web-search/runtime.ts'; import { createCronRunDiagnosticsFromMissingWebSearchProvider } from './src/cron/run-diagnostics.ts'; const providers = [{ id: 'duckduckgo', pluginId: 'duckduckgo', requiresCredential: false }]; const selectedProvider = resolveWebSearchProviderId({ config: {}, providers }); const diagnostics = createCronRunDiagnosticsFromMissingWebSearchProvider({ toolsAllow: ['web_search'], hasWebSearchProvider: Boolean(selectedProvider), nowMs: () => 123456 }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ selectedProvider: selectedProvider || null, diagnostics }, null, 2));"
```

Output:

```json
{
  "selectedProvider": null,
  "diagnostics": {
    "summary": "web_search tool requested in toolsAllow but no web search provider is selected. Configure one with: openclaw configure --section web, or set tools.web.search.provider.",
    "entries": [
      {
        "ts": 123456,
        "source": "cron-preflight",
        "severity": "warn",
        "message": "web_search tool requested in toolsAllow but no web search provider is selected. Configure one with: openclaw configure --section web, or set tools.web.search.provider.",
        "toolName": "web_search"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

**Observed result:** Provider selection was empty and the cron diagnostic contained the warning that tells operators to configure or select a provider.
**Not tested:** A live gateway cron execution with real external search credentials was not run.

## Impact Assessment

- Scope: cron isolated-agent preflight diagnostics and cron diagnostic formatting.
- Downstream: the changed helper is consumed by cron isolated runs and persisted through the existing diagnostics path.
- Edge cases: wildcard-only allowlists, default-derived allowlists, selected providers, and native-search suppression are covered by tests.
- Hard-coded values: no provider availability is assumed; the check uses runtime provider selection.
- Existing fixes: no existing open PR was found for this issue before implementation.

Closes #97654

target.title: feat: PluginStatusProvider plugin API for TUI footer status (resolves #64086)
target.body:
Resolves #64086. Coordinates with #53774 (broader footer-customization tracker).

## What this PR does

Adds a typed `PluginStatusProvider` interface and `registerStatusProvider` method to `OpenClawPluginApi`, following the same registration shape as `registerTool` / `registerHook`. Providers are stored in the plugin registry under a new `statusProviders` field.

```ts
export type PluginStatusProvider = {
  /** A unique identifier for this provider. */
  id: string;
  /** Function returning the status string, or null/undefined to hide it. */
  getStatus: () => string | null | undefined;
};
```

Plugin author usage:

```ts
api.registerStatusProvider({
  id: "contextclaw.savings",
  getStatus: () => `cc: -${savedPercent}%`,
});
```

## Scope

This PR lands the API/registry seam only:

- `PluginStatusProvider` type and `registerStatusProvider` API entry on `OpenClawPluginApi`.
- Plugin registry storage under `statusProviders`.
- API builder and plugin-entry SDK exposure.
- Duplicate, blank, and normalized-id validation.
- Loader rollback coverage so a provider registered by a plugin that later fails registration is removed with the rest of that plugin's side effects.

Explicitly not included:

- TUI render-path wire-up. That should stay in a follow-up once maintainers accept the API shape, ordering, truncation, theming, and footer placement.
- Cost-display footer changes. Those were split out of #64127 and can be revisited under #53774.

## Plugin-author motivation

I'm shipping [ContextClaw](https://github.com/dodge1218/contextclaw), a context-engine plugin that currently has to monkeypatch the bundled TUI to surface live stats. That breaks on OpenClaw release churn. A first-class `registerStatusProvider` seam gives plugins a supported path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: A native plugin can call `api.registerStatusProvider(...)` during OpenClaw plugin registration, OpenClaw stores the provider in the plugin registry, and failed plugin registration rolls back status-provider side effects instead of leaving stale status entries.

Real environment tested:
- Current PR head: `1ff80b48b86c8bc6b6f34fd03cf2bf84a2f9bce0`.
- OpenClaw plugin loader/registry path from this branch.
- Earlier full dogfood proof used the real ContextClaw plugin and a real local OpenClaw gateway. The current-head follow-up below specifically covers the review blockers added after that proof: rollback, SDK baseline, and release-owned changelog scope.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:

```bash
pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check

OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 timeout 240s \
  pnpm exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts \
  src/plugins/registry.status-provider.test.ts \
  src/plugins/loader.test.ts \
  -t 'status provider|rolls back status providers'

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  src/plugins/loader.ts \
  src/plugins/loader.test.ts \
  src/plugins/registry.ts \
  src/plugins/registry.status-provider.test.ts \
  src/plugins/api-builder.ts \
  src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts \
  docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md \
  docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256 \
  CHANGELOG.md

git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
plugin-sdk:api:check: OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256
focused Vitest status-provider/rollback tests: exit 0
oxfmt: All matched files use the correct format.
git diff --check: exit 0
```

Observed result after fix:

- Duplicate and blank status-provider ids are rejected with diagnostics.
- Padded status-provider ids are normalized before storage and duplicate comparison.
- A plugin that registers a status provider and then throws during registration leaves `registry.statusProviders` empty after loader rollback.
- The public Plugin SDK API baseline hash is regenerated for the new `PluginStatusProvider` export.
- The direct `CHANGELOG.md` edit was removed; release-note context stays in the PR body.

Earlier real runtime proof, preserved for the plugin-author path:

```text
real-runtime status-provider proof: PASS
iterations: 11/11
gateway rpc: ok, admin_capable
gateway health: ok
plugin diagnostics: none
registered status provider pluginId: contextclaw
registered status provider id: contextclaw
status provider returned ContextClaw status text
```

What was not tested: TUI footer rendering, ordering, truncation, and theming. This PR intentionally lands only the plugin API/registry seam.

## Coordination with #53774

#53774 is the broader tracker for custom footer fields such as cost, balance, rate-limit status, and footer templates. This PR is the plugin-API piece of that direction. It does not preempt the footer-template decisions.

## Open questions for reviewers

- Naming: `getStatus` vs `render` vs `getText`.
- Return shape: `string | null | undefined` now, or `{ text, color }` upfront.
- Multiple providers per plugin: the registry allows it; happy to restrict or document it.
